L1.area: Database driver layer and schema introspection logic

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where a crafted table name is interpolated directly into SQL query strings used by schema metadata retrieval methods, rather than being passed as a bound parameter. An attacker who can control the table name argument can execute arbitrary SQL statements against the database.

L3.area: Database driver layer and schema introspection logic
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where a crafted table name is interpolated directly into SQL query strings used by schema metadata retrieval methods, rather than being passed as a bound parameter. An attacker who can control the table name argument can execute arbitrary SQL statements against the database.

ground_truth.reason: The sqlite3 driver's MetaColumns(), metaForeignKeys(), and metaIndexes() methods directly interpolate the user-supplied table name into SQL query strings without parameterization, allowing arbitrary SQL injection.
